          Welcome
          I am a Postdoctoral Fellow in the Department of Political
          Science at McMaster
          University. I study comparative politics and political
          methodology.
          Substantively, I am interested in the challenges to
          electoral accountability and representation in the Global
          South. Methodologically, I develop standards to navigate
          research design tradeoffs in experiments and
          quasi-experiments.
          I received my PhD from the Department of Political
          Science at the University
          of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in 2021. Before joining
          McMaster, I was a postdoctoral fellow at the Center for
          Inter-American Policy and Research at Tulane University.
          
        
      
    

    
      
        
          
            
              
            
              Gustavo Diaz

articles.title: Job Market Hub
articles.contents:
Welcome!
This page is for internal use only. If you are not a letter writer, I
encourage you to use the navigation tab on top to learn more about me
and my work.
Upcoming Deadlines



Last update: July 25, 2022. Full list here.
Number of letters to write:


Gisela


Jake


Matt


Michelle


1


7


6


6



Assistant
Professor in Methods - Department of Political Science - University of
MichiganLetters: Gisela, Jake,
Michelle Deadline: August 31, 2022 Delivery: Interfolio Notes:
Focus on Methods of Causal Inference, Big Data, Computational Social
Science, and/or Field Experiments, with strong substantive interests and
expertise in American political behavior, Race and Ethnic Politics,
Campaigns and Elections, International Political Economy, and/or the
Comparative Politics of Developing Countries.  
 Assistant
Professor in Quantitative Methods - Department of Politics - Princeton
UniversityLetters: Jake, Matt Deadline: September 12, 2022 Delivery: Receive email Notes:
NA  
 Assistant
Professor in International/Comparative Politics - Department of
Political Science - Western
UniversityLetters: Jake, Matt,
Michelle Deadline: September 15, 2022 Delivery: Receive email Notes:
We will favour candidates whose teaching and scholarship addresses one
or more of: threats to democracy, environmental politics,
sustainability, climate change, systemic racism, and/or socioeconomic
inequality. The ability to teach empirical research methods will be
considered an asset.  
 Assistant
Professor in Quantitative Methodology - Department of Political Science
- Oklahoma State UniversityLetters:
Jake, Matt, Michelle Deadline: September 15, 2022
Delivery: Interfolio Notes: Preferred methodological techniques include data
visualization, data science and analytics, and machine learning
 
 Assistant
Professor in Comparative Politics - Department of Political Science -
Rice UniversityLetters: Jake, Matt,
Michelle Deadline: September 20, 2022 Delivery: Interfolio Notes:
Chair Randy Stevenson. We are particularly interested in candidates
whose research interests and expertise lie in the cross-national study
of democratic representation. Regional specialization is open, and we
are interested in candidates focusing on mass political behavior and/or
elite behavior and political institutions. Successful candidates will
have advanced skills in quantitative research methods.  
 Assistant
Professor in Comparative Politics - Department of Government - American
UniversityLetters: Jake, Matt,
Michelle Deadline: September 21, 2022 Delivery: Interfolio Notes:
Successful candidates will have strong quantitative skills  
 Assistant
Professor in Comparative Politics - Department of Political Science and
International Relations - University of
DelawareLetters: Jake, Matt, Michelle
Deadline: September 30, 2022 Delivery: Receive email Notes:
Chair Julio Carrion. Research interests in democratization,
de-democratization, democratic backsliding, and/or populism, broadly
construed. The selected candidate will be expected to be able to teach
research methods classes (quantitative or qualitative) at the graduate
level.

articles.title: Research
articles.contents:
Publications
Forthcoming. “Women
Held Back: The Depressing Effect of Institutional and Norms-Based
Barriers on Female Representation in Corrupt Contexts.” In
Norms, Gender and Corruption: Understanding the Nexus, edited
by Ina Kubbe and Ortrun Merkle. Edward Elgar Publishing (with Kelly Senters
Piazza)
2020. “Light
in the Midst of Chaos: COVID-19 and Female Political
Representation.” World Development 136: 105125 (with Kelly Senters
Piazza)
2020. “Survey
Experiments and the Quest for Valid Interpretation.” In The
SAGE Handbook of Research Methods in Political Science and International
Relations, edited by Luigi Curini and Robert Franzese. London:
Sage (with Christopher
Grady and James H.
Kuklinski)
2014. “Civic
Education and Voter Turnout Under Voluntary Voting.”
Política 52(1): 61-91 (In Spanish)
Working Papers
“Assessing the Validity of
Prevalence Estimates in Double List Experiments.”
“Revealing Nearby
Corruption Drives Party Switching: Evidence from Local Level Audits in
Brazil”[Appendix]
“A Model Selection
Approach to Geographic Spillovers.”[Appendix]
[Data]Best poster award. 2019 Latin American Political Methodology
Meeting
Work in Progress
When Should We Use Biased Estimators of the Average Treatment Effect?
(with Jake Bowers and Christopher
Grady)[EGAP resource]
Mayors Alter Spending to Counter the Electoral Consequences of
Increased Monitoring: Evidence from Anti-Corruption Audits in Brazil
